name: Edinburgh
tagline: Historic Charm, World-Class Festivals, and Scottish Grit
description: Edinburgh pairs stunning medieval and Georgian architecture with a thriving cultural scene anchored by the world-famous Fringe Festival. As Scotland's capital, it offers excellent healthcare through the NHS, walkable neighborhoods, and easy access to the Highlands. While weather can be grey and costs are higher than much of Scotland, Edinburgh remains more affordable than London and rewards residents with a uniquely literary, artistic quality of life.

quickFacts.visa: 6-month visitor visa for US citizens; various long-term visa routes available
quickFacts.safetyRating: High – one of the safest cities in the UK
quickFacts.climate: Maritime continental with cool summers and cold, windy winters

housing.description: 1BR flat in Leith, Gorgie, or Dalry — functional but not central

dining.description: Pub meals and casual dining 2x/week; mostly home cooking

healthcare.description: NHS access if registered as resident; covers GP visits and hospital care

transportation.description: Lothian Buses monthly pass; Edinburgh is very walkable

entertainment.description: Free museums, Arthur's Seat hikes, free Fringe shows, library membership

1M.lifestyle: A modest but manageable life in Edinburgh. You will lean on the city's excellent free offerings — world-class museums, stunning hillwalks, and the festival atmosphere that fills the streets each August. Heating costs in winter will be your biggest variable expense, and dining out will be a treat rather than routine.

housing.description: Charming 2BR flat in Stockbridge, Bruntsfield, or Marchmont with period features

dining.description: Regular dining at gastropubs and mid-range restaurants; whisky bar visits

healthcare.description: NHS plus private health insurance for faster specialist access

transportation.description: Bus pass plus occasional taxis and weekend car hire for Highland trips

entertainment.description: Festival tickets, rugby at Murrayfield, gym membership, weekend Highland getaways

2M.lifestyle: Edinburgh becomes genuinely delightful at this level. You can live in one of the city's most charming neighborhoods, enjoy Scotland's exceptional restaurant scene, and afford regular escapes to the Highlands or Scottish islands. The combination of walkability, culture, and natural beauty makes every day feel like a well-curated experience.

housing.description: Spacious 3BR Georgian flat in New Town, or a townhouse in Morningside

dining.description: Fine dining including The Kitchin occasionally, regular whisky tastings, brunch culture

healthcare.description: Comprehensive private health insurance with Bupa or AXA PPP

transportation.description: Owned car for Highland exploring, city bus pass, occasional first-class train to London

entertainment.description: Premium Fringe tickets, golf at historic courses, ski trips to Cairngorms, theater season passes

3M.lifestyle: At this budget you are living Edinburgh's finest everyday life. A beautiful Georgian flat, regular meals at the city's top restaurants, and the freedom to explore Scotland's dramatic landscape whenever the mood strikes. You can fully participate in festival season, join a golf club, and travel to London or the Continent with ease.

housing.description: Premium 4BR townhouse in New Town or a detached home in The Grange with private garden

dining.description: Regular Michelin dining, private chef for entertaining, exclusive whisky club memberships

healthcare.description: VIP private healthcare with same-day specialist appointments and international coverage

transportation.description: Luxury SUV for Highland drives, first-class rail, occasional private hire

entertainment.description: VIP festival packages, exclusive golf club membership, art collecting, premium travel

domesticHelp.description: Weekly housekeeper, gardener, occasional personal assistant

luxuryExtras.description: Highland lodge weekends, first-class European travel, rare whisky collection, philanthropy

5M.lifestyle: You are living as Edinburgh's gentry. A stunning townhouse in the heart of the city, access to Scotland's most exclusive golf courses and whisky collections, and the ability to host memorable dinner parties in a setting that rivals any in Europe. Weekend retreats to a Highland lodge become part of your rhythm.

housing.description: Grand Georgian townhouse in Heriot Row or a country estate on Edinburgh's outskirts

dining.description: Personal chef, private dining experiences, exclusive supper clubs, catered events

healthcare.description: Top-tier concierge medical service with international coverage and wellness retreats

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, private driver, first-class international travel, charter flights

entertainment.description: Private festival events, art patronage, Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo hospitality, yacht charters

domesticHelp.description: Full household staff including housekeeper, cook, personal assistant, and groundskeeper

luxuryExtras.description: Highland estate access, rare whisky cask investments, private jet travel, philanthropic endeavors

10M.lifestyle: At this level Edinburgh offers a life of aristocratic elegance. A grand historic property, full household staff, and the kind of cultural patronage that has defined Edinburgh society for centuries. You move between your city townhouse and Highland retreat with the ease of a Scottish laird, anchored by one of the world's most beautiful and cultured cities.
